prior_round_3_file_audit_carried_forward: _resolve_context removed from posttool-subagent-track.py (was at line 440 in pre-cleanup baseline); audit comment '_resolve_context removed by cycle 20260519-211515 close cleanup' now at lines 440-442. Prior 3 files (allowlist.py / posttool-subagent-track.py / test_allowlist_consolidation.py) UNCHANGED this round; their verdict (0 critical, 2 advisories) carries forward.

scope.files_unchanged_audit_disposition: Prior style-inspector audit verdict (0 critical, 2 advisories on the 3 original files) carried forward unchanged; those files were not re-read this round per OUT_OF_SCOPE contract from the orchestrator.
scope.ac_basis: close.md AC-2.6 cleanliness-of-THIS-diff scoping; line-level default-safe rule
scope.pre_diff_baseline_method: scripts/resolve-close-report.sh: NEW file (git show HEAD:scripts/resolve-close-report.sh errored 'exists on disk but not in HEAD' - acceptable; all 32 lines are introduced_in_diff=true). commands/commit.md: git diff HEAD vs working tree (5 prose lines inserted + 2-line code-block replaced with 1-line invocation, lines 47-54 are the changed-line range).

standards_evaluated.name: no-inline-code
standards_evaluated.rationale: The new code block in commit.md is a SINGLE 1-line bash variable assignment that delegates to scripts/resolve-close-report.sh. This is invocation, not inline logic. The change is a REDUCTION of inline content vs the baseline (2 lines -> 1 line) - exactly the direction Standard 1 prescribes. The path-walk logic is properly extracted to the helper script. Codex consultation confirmed (OBSERVATION_ONLY): 'one-line script invocation/assignment, not inline bash logic under the stated standard.'

standards_evaluated.name: no-hardcoded-values
standards_evaluated.verdict: no critical violations; 1 advisory (raised by codex)

standards_evaluated.name: use-source-venv
standards_evaluated.applicability: shell .sh and command .md that invoke python
standards_evaluated.rationale: resolve-close-report.sh is pure bash, invokes no python. commands/commit.md changed region invokes only bash. Pre-existing 'source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-commit-grant.py' at line 83 is unchanged baseline content.

standards_evaluated.name: integer-step-numbering
standards_evaluated.rationale: commit.md changed region does not introduce step numbering. Existing Step 1..Step 7 sequence is integer and pre-existing.

standards_evaluated.name: meaningful-naming
standards_evaluated.rationale: Filename 'resolve-close-report' is a precise verb-phrase describing the script's contract. No forbidden patterns (enhance-/fast-/optimize-/v2/v3/improved-/better-/new-) appear. All identifiers are descriptive.

standards_evaluated.name: english-only
standards_evaluated.applicability: all source files; full-directory scan exception for commands/+agents/
standards_evaluated.rationale: Grepped scripts/resolve-close-report.sh and commands/commit.md for U+4E00-U+9FFF: 0 hits. Unconditional full-directory scan of commands/ and agents/ returned only PRE-EXISTING verbatim user-binding Chinese quotes in commands/dev.md and commands/close.md (lines 253, 357, 609, 660, 800, 1480, 1482 in dev.md; lines 377-391 in close.md) - these match the Standard-6 verbatim-quote exemption (traceable to docs/dev/ticket-*.md user-binding directives) and are NOT introduced_in_diff. The em-dash (U+2014) at script:30 and commit.md:48 is NOT a Chinese character (codex confirmed: 'Standard 6 only bans Chinese characters').

standards_evaluated.name: merge-similar-scripts
standards_evaluated.rationale: resolve-close-report.sh has a single narrow contract (resolve a close-report path via 3-candidate subproject path-walk). No existing script in scripts/ implements close-report path resolution; nearest neighbors (resolve-dev-report.py, write-commit-grant.py) have distinct contracts. The script's header comment explicitly cross-references the parallel Step 6 changelog-analyst fallback strategy in commit.md, documenting the design relationship.

standards_evaluated.name: concise-documentation
standards_evaluated.rationale: The new 5-line prose addition at commit.md:47-51 is brief technical context (helper purpose + fallback contract + variable post-condition) - within the brief-context pattern the standard explicitly permits. No marketing language ('amazing', 'fast', 'optimized', 'powerful'), no story-style narrative, no repetitive examples, no pseudocode, no excessive explanation markers. Codex confirmed OBSERVATION_ONLY: 'technical context for the helper and fallback contract. I would not call it a concise-documentation violation, though it could be shortened if desired.' Every line carries technical content (fallback ordering / exit-code semantics / post-condition).

standards_evaluated.name: parameterized-scripts
standards_evaluated.verdict: no critical violations; 1 advisory (hardcoded /root/.claude middle candidate per codex)
standards_evaluated.rationale_pass: TASK_ID is a REQUIRED positional arg with ${1:?usage:...} enforcement (fails-loud on missing). CONTROL_ROOT is parameterized via ${CONTROL_ROOT:-/root} env-override with sensible default. CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is taken directly from env (${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-} graceful empty-string fallback). The third (fallback) candidate path is fully parameterized through CONTROL_ROOT. The first (primary) candidate is parameterized through CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR.
standards_evaluated.advisory_rationale: Codex flagged the MIDDLE candidate '/root/.claude/docs/dev/...' as lacking an override mechanism while the first and third candidates are env-parameterized. See advisory_findings[] for full disposition.

standards_evaluated.name: dangling-references
standards_evaluated.rationale: commit.md:54 references '~/.claude/scripts/resolve-close-report.sh' which resolves via /root/.claude/scripts/ symlink to /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/resolve-close-report.sh. The file EXISTS on disk (verified: -rw-r--r-- 1251 bytes). The reference is live, not dangling. No new .py imports introduced (bash script imports nothing).

standards_evaluated.name: no-merge-conflict-markers
standards_evaluated.rationale: Ran ~/.claude/scripts/detect-merge-conflicts.sh /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude and filtered for the 2 changed files - 0 hits. (Repo-wide pre-existing markers in INDEX.md / hooks/README.md exist but are not in the 2 cycle-diff files; not introduced_in_diff per AC-2.6.)

advisory_findings_this_round.finding: The middle candidate in the path-walk loop hardcodes '/root/.claude/docs/dev/close-report-${TASK_ID}.md' with no env-override mechanism, while the first candidate is parameterized through CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR and the third through CONTROL_ROOT. Codex raised this as the SOLE adversarial blocker in its consultation. The literal '/root/.claude/' IS the well-known dot-claude install location on this control box (symlink target of /root/.claude -> /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude per /root/.claude/CLAUDE.md 'Nested .claude Repo' section), so the value is stable on this topology - but the inconsistency in parameterization style is a legitimate Standard-2/9 concern that codex correctly identified.
advisory_findings_this_round.recommendation: Apply codex's PROPOSED_FIX: add 'CLAUDE_DOT_ROOT="${CLAUDE_DOT_ROOT:-${CONTROL_ROOT}/.claude}"' near the existing CONTROL_ROOT assignment; replace the middle-candidate literal with '${CLAUDE_DOT_ROOT}/docs/dev/close-report-${TASK_ID}.md'. Also rephrase the header comment 'fall back to /root/docs/dev/' to use symbolic CONTROL_ROOT/docs/dev/.
advisory_findings_this_round.disposition_rationale: Logged as ADVISORY rather than CRITICAL because (a) Standard 2 explicitly exempts 'Constants that genuinely never change' and /root/.claude/ IS the stable dot-claude install location on this server (documented in CLAUDE.md global instructions), (b) the actual fallback path (third candidate, used when neither CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR nor the nested location resolves) IS parameterized via CONTROL_ROOT - environment-override capability exists for the failure-mode error-message path, (c) the middle candidate's role is a TACTICAL fix for the specific nested-repo case that caused the prior CLOSE: NO (Bullet 1 dissent), so its existence is the entire point of the fix-forward. Codex classified this as 'blocker' under Standard 2 but acknowledged the override convention exists for the other two; reasonable people disagree on whether convention-stable paths count under the 'genuinely never change' exemption. The fix is small (2 lines added) and SHOULD be applied as cleanup-followup, but does NOT meet the bar for 'critical violation that blocks close-cleanliness' per AC-2.6 default-safe rule. close.md and the orchestrator may override this judgment if they choose.

advisory_findings_carried_forward_from_p.carryforward_note: From prior /close round; file unchanged this round.
advisory_findings_carried_forward_from_p.finding: Magic number K=3 hardcoded inline: 'end = min(len(canonical), start + 3)  # K=3 bounded window'. BA-ticket-mandated constant; inline literal-3 with only an end-of-line comment is weaker than a named module-level constant.
advisory_findings_carried_forward_from_p.recommendation: Introduce module-level constant 'SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW = 3  # BA cycle 20260519-211515 Item H' and replace 'start + 3' with 'start + SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW'.
advisory_findings_carried_forward_from_p.disposition_rationale: Advisory only - does not block close cleanliness.

advisory_findings_carried_forward_from_p.carryforward_note: From prior /close round; file unchanged this round.
advisory_findings_carried_forward_from_p.finding: New AC-D1 regression tests embed the literal grant-file path '/tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{test_sid}.json' directly in test bodies; test-fixture coupling that matches the production grant-file contract literal in hooks/lib/allowlist.py:120.
advisory_findings_carried_forward_from_p.recommendation: Optionally extract a shared helper 'lib.allowlist.grant_path_for(sid: str) -> Path' and use it from both production sites AND test fixtures. Not required for close-cleanliness.
advisory_findings_carried_forward_from_p.disposition_rationale: Mirroring the pre-existing test pattern is acceptable - refactoring is a separate follow-up cycle concern.

observations_this_round.finding: Codex confirmed that the inline assignment 'CLOSE_REPORT=$(bash ~/.claude/scripts/resolve-close-report.sh "$TASK_ID") || true' is a one-line script invocation/assignment - NOT inline bash logic under Standard 1. The '|| true' is documented error-contract handling for the helper's intentional dual-output pattern.
observations_this_round.recommendation: (No change required.) Logged as transparency that the inline question was considered and explicitly cleared.
observations_this_round.disposition_rationale: Codex explicitly downgraded to OBSERVATION_ONLY; not a Standard 1 violation.

observations_this_round.finding: Script uses 'set -u' but not 'set -e' or 'set -o pipefail'. Codex confirmed: 'Not covered by Standards 1-11. No flag.'
observations_this_round.recommendation: (Out of inspector scope - functional/safety follow-up if desired.) The script's exit-code contract is explicit (echo+exit 0 on hit, echo+exit 1 on miss); the for-loop has no pipelines or compound failures that pipefail would catch. set -e would change the contract because the [ -n $candidate ] guard for the unset-CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR case would interact unpredictably.
observations_this_round.disposition_rationale: Outside the 11 enumerated style standards. Codex explicitly classified as OBSERVATION_ONLY.

observations_this_round.finding: Prose addition is 5 lines vs the standard's brief-context guideline. Codex confirmed: 'I would not call it a concise-documentation violation, though it could be shortened if desired.'
observations_this_round.recommendation: (Soft, not blocking) Consider compressing to 2-3 lines if a follow-up cycle revisits Step 3. Not required for close-cleanliness.
observations_this_round.disposition_rationale: Every line carries technical content (fallback ordering / exit-code semantics / variable post-condition). Codex explicitly classified as OBSERVATION_ONLY.

observations_this_round.finding: Script echoes a path AND exits 0 on success; echoes the fallback path AND exits 1 on miss. Caller distinguishes via exit code. Codex confirmed: 'documented in the script header and used intentionally with exit status. Not a style-standard violation.'
observations_this_round.recommendation: (No change required.) Header comment lines 11-14 explicitly document the dual-output contract.
observations_this_round.disposition_rationale: Pattern is intentional and documented. Not a standards violation.

observations_this_round.absent_from_baseline: partial (commit.md em-dash also appears in pre-existing prose elsewhere in the file; script line 30 is new)
observations_this_round.finding: Both files contain em-dashes (U+2014). Codex confirmed: 'Standard 6 only bans Chinese characters (U+4E00-U+9FFF); this is not a violation.'
observations_this_round.disposition_rationale: Em-dash is not a Chinese character. Standard 6 scope is U+4E00-U+9FFF only.

observations_carried_forward_from_prior_.standard: documentation-accuracy (not one of the 11 critical standards)
observations_carried_forward_from_prior_.finding: TestReadGrant class docstring still says 'exact_or_substr semantics' but cycle 20260519-211515 changed read_grant to exact_only. Outside diff-overlap line range -> introduced_in_diff=false per AC-2.6.
observations_carried_forward_from_prior_.disposition_rationale: Not a violation of any of the 11 enumerated standards. Logged for transparency only.

observations_carried_forward_from_prior_.standard: exception-handling (functional risk, NOT a style standard)
observations_carried_forward_from_prior_.finding: Broad 'except Exception: return None' in _try_load_contract is pre-existing baseline; cycle's new contract-presence-first routing makes the failure mode more visible.
observations_carried_forward_from_prior_.disposition_rationale: Pre-existing code shape; outside diff-overlap; functional concern not in the 11 style standards. QA territory.

observations_carried_forward_from_prior_.finding: Codex explicitly downgraded the long helper name from possible naming concern to OBSERVATION_ONLY: 'long but precise; do not flag it as a blocker.'

verdict.summary: NO NEW CRITICAL VIOLATIONS introduced by the SECOND-/close fix-forward diff (scripts/resolve-close-report.sh NEW + commands/commit.md Step 3 modification). One NEW advisory raised by codex (Standard 2/9: middle path-walk candidate /root/.claude/docs/dev/ is hardcoded literal vs the parameterized first and third candidates) with concrete PROPOSED_FIX - logged for cleanup-followup but does NOT block close-cleanliness per AC-2.6 default-safe rule (the path is conventionally stable on this server topology and codex acknowledged the convention). Five observations all logged for transparency. Prior-round 3-file audit verdict (0 critical, 2 advisories on allowlist.py / posttool-subagent-track.py / test_allowlist_consolidation.py) carries forward UNCHANGED - those files were not modified this round. Aggregate cycle-level critical violation count: ZERO. close_cleanliness_gate=pass.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 6 challenges to the draft verdict. (1) K=3 advisory: report verdict was internally inconsistent ('NO NEW VIOLATIONS' but described an advisory) — corrected. (2) Constant name K_WINDOW is itself opaque — adopted SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW instead. (3) /tmp/... paths in new tests are newly-added hardcoded path literals; added as second advisory with introduced_in_diff=true. (4) _try_load_contract exception swallowing: pre-existing — logged as observation_only (out-of-scope functional risk). (5) Stale TestReadGrant class docstring: line 113 outside diff-overlap → introduced_in_diff=false per AC-2.6 → logged as observation_only. (6) Helper name length: explicitly downgraded by codex to OBSERVATION_ONLY.

findings.issue: Verdict was internally inconsistent: 'NO NEW VIOLATIONS' but text described an advisory K=3 finding.
findings.proposed_fix: Restate verdict as 'NO NEW CRITICAL VIOLATIONS; 1 advisory Standard-2/9 finding: replace inline K=3 with module constant SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW = 3.'
findings.rationale: Correct logical inconsistency. The verdict.summary and the advisory_findings array are now coherent: 0 critical, 2 advisories.

findings.proposed_fix: Use SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW = 3 or TYPED_LOOKUP_LOOKAHEAD = 3 in the recommendation, not K_WINDOW.
findings.rationale: Self-consistency: if we recommend a name in a Standard-5 sense we must follow Standard 5's spirit. Recommendation now reads SUBAGENT_TYPED_LOOKUP_WINDOW.

findings.issue: Draft claimed 'No new hardcoded paths introduced' but new tests at lines 158, 177 add /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{test_sid}.json literals.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword Standard-2 to: 'New tests add /tmp allowlist fixture paths matching the production grant-file contract; accepted as test-fixture coupling, but could be centralized via helper allowlist_path(sid).'
findings.rationale: Correctly factual. Added as second advisory finding with introduced_in_diff=true and matching diff_overlap=true.

findings.issue: _try_load_contract exception swallowing is risky given new contract-presence-first routing.
findings.proposed_fix: Add an out-of-scope functional-risk note: 'Broad contract load exception swallowing may fail open into legacy path; distinguish absent contract from load failure, and no-write on load failure.'
findings.rationale: Pre-existing code shape (lines 279-287); outside diff-overlap; functional concern not in the 11 style standards. Logged in observations[] for orchestrator transparency.

findings.issue: Stale TestReadGrant class docstring at line 113 still says 'exact_or_substr semantics' while the cycle changes read_grant to exact_only.
findings.proposed_fix: Change docstring to 'Unit tests for read_grant (public API, exact_only literal semantics).'
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly noted 'Not one of the 11 critical standards, but it is a documentation accuracy miss.' Per AC-2.6 line-level rule, line 113 is outside diff-overlap → introduced_in_diff=false → cannot be flagged as cycle-introduced critical. Logged in observations[] as soft transparency note.
Codex explicitly downgraded this: 'long but precise; do not flag it as a blocker.' OBSERVATION_ONLY per codex's own classification.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 6 codex findings addressed. 2 applied (K=3 advisory + constant name rewording + new test-path advisory). 3 logged as observations (exception-handling functional risk, stale docstring, helper-name length). 0 rejected. Verdict.summary now consistent with advisory_findings array; zero critical_violations; close_cleanliness_gate=pass.
